meta.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
meta.philosophy: Creative and Technology Studies Syllabus for ECE Levels 1 and 2 is the combination of Art and Design, Design and Technology, Information and Communications Technology, Music and Physical Education. It also integrates Social Studies. The focus of Creative and Technology Studies at Early Childhood Education is to promote creativity, critical thinking, problem solving and positive inter-relationships helping children develop confidence, awareness of space and assertiveness. Section B covers content for Level 2 (4-5 years).
meta.competence_learning: The approach to teaching and learning is learner-centred. Learning in school must build on the learner's prior knowledge and experience. Each learner has individual needs and abilities shaped by their life experiences. The teacher must determine the needs of the learners and shape the learning experience appropriately. Methods must be varied but flexible within well-structured sequences of lessons.
goals: Develop collaboration by demonstrating ability to physically interact at play and working with peers
Build analytical thinking by applying analytical skills and identifying patterns
Foster critical thinking by identifying tools found in the immediate environment
Develop communication by expressing oneself using different materials
Promote creativity and innovation through drawing, singing, dancing and making items
Build digital literacy by identifying and using basic digital tools
Develop problem solving using patterns and relations
Promote environmental sustainability by disposing trash in designated places
Develop entrepreneurship through role-play saving and entrepreneurial activities

specific_outcomes: Practise safety in the school environment
Observe safety rules in specialised rooms (eating in the lab, running)
Practise safety in the playground (space up, sharp objects)
Practise road safety
Interpret road signs (pedestrian crossing, traffic light)
Store tools in designated places
Pack tools in right places
Recognise dangers of interacting with strangers
Mention the dangers of following strangers and getting presents
Role-play the dangers of interacting with strangers
Preserve food
Talk about ways of preserving food (cooking of food, drying, refrigeration, smoking)
Preserve foods (vegetables, fruits)
Practise personal hygiene
Demonstrate good grooming activities (trimming nails, washing their face, brushing, washing hands, clean clothing, correct use of toiletries)
Care for the environment
Pick waste (bottles for recycling)
Throw litter in right places
Show ways of caring for the environment
Make toys from waste (skipping rope, reducing waste, use of water)
Making toys from recycled materials
Explore the various sources of heat
Identify sources of heat (sun, fire, stove, iron, heater)
Discuss the dangers of heat
Role-play how to stay safe from various sources of heat
Explore the uses of different sources of heat
Use ICT devices
Watch morally right educational cartoons and music
Play simple computer games
Use a camera to capture pictures
Use notepads to draw simple graphical drawings
Sending and receiving phone calls
Practise good manners
Discuss ways of showing good manners to people
Apply good manners: using polite words and phrases when talking to people, respecting other people's things, telling the truth in all your actions and dealings
Practise good eating habits
Demonstrate good eating habits
Discuss good eating habits (taking when enough for oneself, eating with the mouth closed, considering others, sharing foods)
Practise good eating habits
Develop various locomotor skills
Perform various movement and control: walking on straight line
Perform jumping and balancing activities (frog jumping, hole game, alternating jump from height 30cm, step over rope 30cm high, climbing up stairs using alternating feet)
Develop various non-locomotor skills
Perform static body movements: pulling, twisting, stretching
Demonstrate throwing ball overhead
Perform various body movements: rolling, turning
Perform posture balancing activities: sitting, standing
Demonstrate jumping on one place (number of times)
Manipulate different materials in the immediate environment
Arrange domino pieces
Stringing using clay
Develop eye-hand and eye-foot coordination
Perform eye-hand and eye-foot coordination activities: throwing, kicking, catching, lacing
Perform throwing and catching activities (throw object to target, catch object with both hands, throw under and over on one leg)
Demonstrate kicking and rolling activities (timing, kicking a stationary ball to a target, rolling to a target)
Develop various fine motor skills
Manipulate different objects to build a tower (pieces, build blocks, plastic lids)
Perform dressing activities (dressing oneself, zipping, buttoning, tying shoelaces)
Practise scooping activities (measuring and size estimation)
Perform clipping activities (holding, clipping, opening/closing, use of tongs/tweezers)
Practise music and movement
Practise music and movement by singing familiar songs, controlling breath control, diction, volume projection
Perform coordinated voice activities
Respond to different music, rhythm and tempo
Perform free dance
Practise full control to balance body
Perform Guided rhythm activities: rhythm and melody
Play different instruments to explore music (woodblocks, shakers, drums, rhythm sticks, flute, mouth organ, piano)
Dramatise different scenarios (family, traditional ceremonies, buying and selling)
Role-play adult lives
Perform traditional and conventional games
Perform traditional games (nsolo, chiyato, waida, chidunu)
Perform conventional games (football, netball)
Utilise different materials
Identify different materials in their locality
Sort different objects according to their materials (soil, mud, glass, bricks, blocks, plastic and metal)
Utilise different materials to make toys
Drawing and colouring
Draw different straight and zig-zag lines
Practise colouring activities: shapes and patterns
Draw familiar objects (cup, ball and pot of Zambia)
Use geometrical shapes to make shapes and patterns
Construct structured shapes/building using variety of building materials (making of saving boxes)
Practise tracing of objects
Pattern making: making patterns using materials (okra, mopane, banana stem)
Make different woven items (hats, mats, paper crafts)
Make familiar artifacts by holing, pressing and pinning familiar things from their locality (pots, dolls, cups, dogs, houses, people)
Recognise materials for making collage/mosaic pictures (glue, beads, paper and fabric, bottle tops)
Make various pictures using collage and mosaic techniques
